chr_premature_death.long_name: Years of Potential Life Lost Rate
chr_premature_death.short_description: Years of potential life lost before age 75 per 100,000 population (age-adjusted).
chr_premature_death.long_description: Years of potential life lost (YPLL) before age 75 per 100,000 population, age-adjusted. Based on death records from the National Center for Health Statistics. Higher values indicate more premature death in the community.
chr_premature_death.statement: In {location}, the premature death rate was {value} years of potential life lost per 100,000 population.
chr_premature_death.unit: Years of potential life lost per 100,000

chr_life_expectancy.short_description: Average number of years a person can expect to live, expressed in years.
chr_life_expectancy.long_description: Average life expectancy at birth in years, estimated using the Chiang method applied to age-specific death rates. Derived from National Center for Health Statistics death data. Lower values indicate shorter average lifespans in the community.
chr_life_expectancy.statement: In {location}, the average life expectancy was {value} years.

chr_poor_or_fair_health.long_name: Percentage Reporting Poor or Fair Health
chr_poor_or_fair_health.short_description: Percentage of adults reporting fair or poor general health status.
chr_poor_or_fair_health.long_description: Percentage of adults who report fair or poor general health. Based on the Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS) survey asking respondents to rate their own general health.
chr_poor_or_fair_health.statement: In {location}, {value} of adults reported poor or fair health.

chr_poor_physical_health_days.long_name: Average Number of Physically Unhealthy Days Per Month
chr_poor_physical_health_days.short_description: Average number of days per month adults report poor physical health.
chr_poor_physical_health_days.long_description: Average number of physically unhealthy days reported in the past 30 days among adults. Measured via BRFSS survey. Higher values indicate greater burden of poor physical health days.
chr_poor_physical_health_days.statement: In {location}, adults reported an average of {value} poor physical health days per month.

chr_poor_mental_health_days.long_name: Average Number of Mentally Unhealthy Days Per Month
chr_poor_mental_health_days.short_description: Average number of days per month adults report poor mental health.
chr_poor_mental_health_days.long_description: Average number of mentally unhealthy days reported in the past 30 days among adults. Measured via BRFSS survey. Higher values indicate greater burden of poor mental health days.
chr_poor_mental_health_days.statement: In {location}, adults reported an average of {value} poor mental health days per month.

chr_low_birth_weight.long_name: Percentage of Live Births with Low Birth Weight
chr_low_birth_weight.short_description: Percentage of live births with birth weight less than 2,500 grams.
chr_low_birth_weight.long_description: Percentage of live births with birth weight below 2,500 grams. Based on National Center for Health Statistics natality data. Low birth weight is associated with infant mortality and developmental issues.
chr_low_birth_weight.statement: In {location}, {value} of live births had low birth weight.

chr_premature_age_adj_mortality.short_description: Age-adjusted mortality rate for deaths before age 75, per 100,000 population.
chr_premature_age_adj_mortality.long_description: Age-adjusted rate of deaths before age 75 per 100,000 population. Based on National Center for Health Statistics mortality data. This measure captures premature mortality while accounting for differences in age distribution across counties.
chr_premature_age_adj_mortality.statement: In {location}, the premature age-adjusted mortality rate was {value} per 100,000 population.

chr_child_mortality.short_description: Deaths among children ages 0–17 per 100,000 population.
chr_child_mortality.long_description: Age-adjusted rate of deaths among children and youth ages 0–17 per 100,000 population. Based on National Center for Health Statistics mortality data.
chr_child_mortality.statement: In {location}, the child mortality rate was {value} deaths per 100,000 children.

chr_infant_mortality.short_description: Deaths among infants under age 1 per 1,000 live births.
chr_infant_mortality.long_description: Infant mortality rate expressed as deaths per 1,000 live births. Based on linked birth/infant death data from the National Center for Health Statistics.
chr_infant_mortality.statement: In {location}, the infant mortality rate was {value} deaths per 1,000 live births.

chr_frequent_physical_distress.short_description: Percentage of adults reporting 14 or more physically unhealthy days per month.
chr_frequent_physical_distress.long_description: Percentage of adults reporting 14 or more physically unhealthy days in the past 30 days. Based on BRFSS survey data.
chr_frequent_physical_distress.statement: In {location}, {value} of adults reported frequent physical distress.

chr_frequent_mental_distress.short_description: Percentage of adults reporting 14 or more mentally unhealthy days per month.
chr_frequent_mental_distress.long_description: Percentage of adults reporting 14 or more mentally unhealthy days in the past 30 days. Based on BRFSS survey data.
chr_frequent_mental_distress.statement: In {location}, {value} of adults reported frequent mental distress.

chr_adult_smoking.long_name: Percentage of Adults Who Currently Smoke
chr_adult_smoking.short_description: Percentage of adults who currently smoke cigarettes.
chr_adult_smoking.long_description: Percentage of adults who report currently smoking cigarettes (every day or some days). Based on BRFSS survey data. Smoking is a leading cause of preventable disease and death.

chr_adult_obesity.short_description: Percentage of adults with a body mass index (BMI) of 30 or more.
chr_adult_obesity.long_description: Percentage of the adult population (age 20 and older) that reports a body mass index (BMI) greater than or equal to 30 kg/m2. Based on BRFSS survey data modeled by CDC.

chr_physical_inactivity.long_name: Percentage of Adults Reporting Physical Inactivity
chr_physical_inactivity.short_description: Percentage of adults reporting no leisure-time physical activity.
chr_physical_inactivity.long_description: Percentage of adults aged 20 and over reporting no leisure-time physical activity. Based on BRFSS survey data modeled by CDC.
chr_physical_inactivity.statement: In {location}, {value} of adults are physically inactive.

chr_excessive_drinking.long_name: Percentage of Adults Reporting Excessive Drinking
chr_excessive_drinking.short_description: Percentage of adults reporting binge or heavy drinking.
chr_excessive_drinking.long_description: Percentage of adults who report binge drinking (4+ drinks for women, 5+ for men on a single occasion) or heavy drinking (8+ drinks per week for women, 15+ for men). Based on BRFSS survey data.
chr_excessive_drinking.statement: In {location}, {value} of adults report excessive drinking.

chr_drug_overdose_deaths.short_description: Age-adjusted rate of drug overdose deaths per 100,000 population.
chr_drug_overdose_deaths.long_description: Age-adjusted rate of drug overdose deaths per 100,000 population. Based on death certificate data from the National Center for Health Statistics. Includes overdose deaths involving both illicit drugs and prescription medications.
chr_drug_overdose_deaths.statement: In {location}, the drug overdose death rate was {value} per 100,000 population.

chr_teen_births.long_description: Number of births per 1,000 female population ages 15–19. Based on National Center for Health Statistics natality data. Teen births are associated with adverse maternal and child health outcomes.
chr_teen_births.statement: In {location}, there were {value} teen births per 1,000 females ages 15–19.

chr_sexually_transmitted_infections.short_description: New cases of chlamydia per 100,000 population.
chr_sexually_transmitted_infections.long_description: Number of newly diagnosed chlamydia cases per 100,000 population. Based on CDC STI Surveillance data. Chlamydia is used as a proxy for overall STI burden as it is the most commonly reported STI.
chr_sexually_transmitted_infections.statement: In {location}, the chlamydia rate was {value} cases per 100,000 population.

chr_suicides.short_description: Age-adjusted rate of deaths due to suicide per 100,000 population.
chr_suicides.long_description: Age-adjusted rate of deaths due to intentional self-harm (suicide) per 100,000 population. Based on death certificate data from the National Center for Health Statistics.
chr_suicides.statement: In {location}, the suicide rate was {value} per 100,000 population.

chr_insufficient_sleep.long_name: Percentage of Adults Reporting Insufficient Sleep
chr_insufficient_sleep.short_description: Percentage of adults reporting fewer than 7 hours of sleep on average.
chr_insufficient_sleep.long_description: Percentage of adults who report sleeping less than 7 hours on average per 24-hour period. Based on BRFSS survey data.
chr_insufficient_sleep.statement: In {location}, {value} of adults report insufficient sleep.

chr_uninsured.short_description: Percentage of population under age 65 without health insurance.
chr_uninsured.long_description: Percentage of the population under age 65 that does not have health insurance coverage. Based on Small Area Health Insurance Estimates (SAHIE) from the U.S. Census Bureau.
chr_uninsured.statement: In {location}, {value} of the population under 65 lack health insurance.

chr_uninsured_adults.long_name: Percentage of Adults Without Health Insurance
chr_uninsured_adults.short_description: Percentage of adults ages 18–64 without health insurance.
chr_uninsured_adults.long_description: Percentage of adults ages 18–64 who lack health insurance coverage. Based on Small Area Health Insurance Estimates (SAHIE) from the U.S. Census Bureau.
chr_uninsured_adults.statement: In {location}, {value} of adults ages 18–64 lack health insurance.

chr_uninsured_children.long_name: Percentage of Children Without Health Insurance
chr_uninsured_children.short_description: Percentage of children under age 19 without health insurance.
chr_uninsured_children.long_description: Percentage of children under age 19 who lack health insurance coverage. Based on Small Area Health Insurance Estimates (SAHIE) from the U.S. Census Bureau.
chr_uninsured_children.statement: In {location}, {value} of children under age 19 lack health insurance.

chr_primary_care_physicians.long_name: Ratio of Population to Primary Care Physicians
chr_primary_care_physicians.short_description: Ratio of population to primary care physicians.
chr_primary_care_physicians.long_description: Ratio of the total population to the number of primary care physicians (MDs and DOs) providing patient care. Lower values indicate better access to primary care. Based on data from Dartmouth Atlas of Health Care.
chr_primary_care_physicians.statement: In {location}, there is one primary care physician for every {value} residents.

chr_mental_health_providers.long_name: Ratio of Population to Mental Health Providers
chr_mental_health_providers.short_description: Ratio of population to mental health providers.
chr_mental_health_providers.long_description: Ratio of the total population to the number of mental health providers, including psychiatrists, psychologists, licensed clinical social workers, counselors, and marriage and family therapists. Lower values indicate better access. Based on data from CMS.
chr_mental_health_providers.statement: In {location}, there is one mental health provider for every {value} residents.

chr_preventable_hospital_stays.short_description: Rate of hospital stays for ambulatory-care sensitive conditions per 100,000 Medicare enrollees.
chr_preventable_hospital_stays.long_description: Discharge rate for ambulatory care-sensitive conditions per 100,000 Medicare enrollees. Based on Medicare inpatient claims data from Dartmouth Atlas of Health Care. These are conditions that can often be managed outside of a hospital with appropriate primary care.
chr_preventable_hospital_stays.statement: In {location}, the preventable hospital stay rate was {value} per 100,000 Medicare enrollees.

chr_flu_vaccinations.short_description: Percentage of Medicare enrollees who received an annual influenza vaccination.
chr_flu_vaccinations.long_description: Percentage of fee-for-service Medicare enrollees that had an annual influenza vaccination. Based on Medicare claims data from the Dartmouth Atlas of Health Care.
chr_flu_vaccinations.statement: In {location}, {value} of Medicare enrollees received an annual flu vaccination.

chr_diabetes_prevalence.short_description: Percentage of adults with diagnosed diabetes.
chr_diabetes_prevalence.long_description: Percentage of adults aged 20 and older with diagnosed diabetes. Based on BRFSS survey data modeled by CDC.
chr_diabetes_prevalence.statement: In {location}, {value} of adults have diagnosed diabetes.

chr_hiv_prevalence.short_description: Number of people diagnosed with HIV per 100,000 population.
chr_hiv_prevalence.long_description: Prevalence of HIV (number of people diagnosed with HIV not yet deceased) per 100,000 population. Based on CDC HIV Surveillance Report data.
chr_hiv_prevalence.statement: In {location}, the HIV prevalence was {value} cases per 100,000 population.

chr_mammography_screening.long_name: Percentage of Female Medicare Enrollees Who Had a Mammogram
chr_mammography_screening.short_description: Percentage of female Medicare enrollees who received a mammography screening.
chr_mammography_screening.long_description: Percentage of female Medicare enrollees ages 65–74 who received a mammography screening in the past two years. Based on Medicare claims data from the Dartmouth Atlas of Health Care.
chr_mammography_screening.statement: In {location}, {value} of eligible female Medicare enrollees received a mammography screening.

chr_unemployment.short_description: Percentage of the labor force that is unemployed.
chr_unemployment.long_description: Percentage of the civilian labor force that is unemployed but seeking work. Based on Bureau of Labor Statistics Local Area Unemployment Statistics (LAUS).

chr_median_household_income.short_description: Median annual household income in dollars.
chr_median_household_income.long_description: Median annual household income (in dollars). Based on American Community Survey 5-year estimates from the U.S. Census Bureau.
chr_median_household_income.statement: In {location}, the median household income was {value}.

chr_children_in_poverty.short_description: Percentage of children under age 18 living in poverty.
chr_children_in_poverty.long_description: Percentage of children under age 18 living in households with income below the federal poverty level. Based on U.S. Census Bureau Small Area Income and Poverty Estimates (SAIPE).
chr_children_in_poverty.statement: In {location}, {value} of children live in poverty.

chr_income_inequality.short_description: Ratio of household income at the 80th percentile to the 20th percentile.
chr_income_inequality.long_description: Ratio of household income at the 80th percentile to household income at the 20th percentile. Based on American Community Survey 5-year estimates. Higher values indicate greater income inequality.
chr_income_inequality.statement: In {location}, the income at the 80th percentile was {value} times the income at the 20th percentile.

chr_some_college.short_description: Percentage of adults ages 25–44 with some post-secondary education.
chr_some_college.long_description: Percentage of adults ages 25–44 that report having some post-secondary education. Based on American Community Survey 5-year estimates from the U.S. Census Bureau.
chr_some_college.statement: In {location}, {value} of adults ages 25–44 have some post-secondary education.

chr_high_school_completion.long_name: Percentage of Adults with High School Diploma
chr_high_school_completion.short_description: Percentage of adults ages 25 and over with a high school diploma or equivalent.
chr_high_school_completion.long_description: Percentage of adults ages 25 and over who have a high school diploma or equivalent. Based on American Community Survey 5-year estimates from the U.S. Census Bureau.
chr_high_school_completion.statement: In {location}, {value} of adults ages 25 and over have completed high school.

chr_high_school_graduation.short_description: Percentage of ninth-grade cohort that graduates high school on time.
chr_high_school_graduation.long_description: Percentage of incoming ninth-grade students who graduate on time (within four years). Based on EDFacts data collected by the Department of Education. The 4-year cohort graduation rate is also known as the Adjusted Cohort Graduation Rate (ACGR).
chr_high_school_graduation.statement: In {location}, {value} of high school students graduate on time.

chr_food_insecurity.long_name: Percentage of Population Experiencing Food Insecurity
chr_food_insecurity.short_description: Percentage of the population experiencing food insecurity.
chr_food_insecurity.long_description: Percentage of the population who lack reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food. Based on modeled estimates from Feeding America's Map the Meal Gap study.
chr_food_insecurity.statement: In {location}, {value} of the population experiences food insecurity.

chr_disconnected_youth.short_description: Percentage of teens and young adults ages 16–24 not in school or work.
chr_disconnected_youth.long_description: Percentage of teens and young adults ages 16–24 who are neither enrolled in school nor employed. Based on American Community Survey 5-year estimates from the U.S. Census Bureau.
chr_disconnected_youth.statement: In {location}, {value} of youth ages 16–24 are disconnected (not in school or work).

chr_air_pollution_pm.short_description: Average daily density of fine particulate matter (PM2.5) in micrograms per cubic meter.
chr_air_pollution_pm.long_description: Average daily density of fine particulate matter (PM2.5) in micrograms per cubic meter. Based on EPA monitoring station data and satellite-based estimates from CDC. Higher values indicate worse air quality.
chr_air_pollution_pm.statement: In {location}, the average daily fine particulate matter level was {value} micrograms per cubic meter.

chr_broadband_access.long_name: Percentage with Access to Broadband Internet
chr_broadband_access.short_description: Percentage of households with broadband internet subscriptions.
chr_broadband_access.long_description: Percentage of households with broadband (high-speed) internet subscription. Based on American Community Survey 5-year estimates from the U.S. Census Bureau.
chr_broadband_access.statement: In {location}, {value} of households have broadband internet access.

chr_exercise_access.long_name: Percentage with Adequate Access to Exercise Opportunities
chr_exercise_access.short_description: Percentage of the population with adequate access to locations for physical activity.
chr_exercise_access.long_description: Percentage of the population with access to places for physical activity such as parks, fitness/recreation centers, or similar recreational amenities. Based on data from the ESRI Business Analyst database.
chr_exercise_access.statement: In {location}, {value} of the population has access to exercise opportunities.

chr_severe_housing_problems.short_description: Percentage of households with at least one of four housing problems.
chr_severe_housing_problems.long_description: Percentage of households with at least one of four housing problems: overcrowding, high housing costs, lack of kitchen facilities, or lack of plumbing facilities. Based on American Housing Survey 5-year estimates from the U.S. Census Bureau.
chr_severe_housing_problems.statement: In {location}, {value} of households have severe housing problems.

chr_injury_deaths.short_description: Age-adjusted rate of deaths due to injury per 100,000 population.
chr_injury_deaths.long_description: Age-adjusted rate of deaths due to injury (unintentional and intentional) per 100,000 population. Based on death certificate data from the National Center for Health Statistics.
chr_injury_deaths.statement: In {location}, the injury death rate was {value} per 100,000 population.

chr_homicides.short_description: Age-adjusted rate of deaths due to homicide per 100,000 population.
chr_homicides.long_description: Age-adjusted rate of deaths due to homicide per 100,000 population. Based on death certificate data from the National Center for Health Statistics.
chr_homicides.statement: In {location}, the homicide rate was {value} per 100,000 population.

chr_firearm_fatalities.short_description: Age-adjusted rate of deaths due to firearms per 100,000 population.
chr_firearm_fatalities.long_description: Age-adjusted rate of deaths due to firearms (including homicide, suicide, and unintentional injury) per 100,000 population. Based on death certificate data from the National Center for Health Statistics.
chr_firearm_fatalities.statement: In {location}, the firearm fatality rate was {value} per 100,000 population.

chr_motor_vehicle_crash_deaths.short_description: Age-adjusted rate of motor vehicle crash deaths per 100,000 population.
chr_motor_vehicle_crash_deaths.long_description: Age-adjusted rate of deaths due to motor vehicle crashes per 100,000 population. Based on death certificate data from the National Center for Health Statistics.
chr_motor_vehicle_crash_deaths.statement: In {location}, the motor vehicle crash death rate was {value} per 100,000 population.

chr_residential_segregation.short_description: Index of dissimilarity measuring residential segregation between Black and White residents.
chr_residential_segregation.long_description: Index of dissimilarity between Black and White county residents. Ranges from 0 (complete integration) to 100 (complete segregation). Represents the percentage of the Black (or White) population that would need to move to achieve an even racial distribution. Based on American Community Survey data.
chr_residential_segregation.statement: In {location}, the residential segregation index was {value}.

chr_social_associations.short_description: Number of membership associations per 10,000 population.
chr_social_associations.long_description: Number of membership associations per 10,000 population, including civic organizations, bowling centers, golf courses, fitness centers, sports organizations, religious organizations, and labor organizations. Based on County Business Patterns data from the U.S. Census Bureau. Higher values indicate more opportunities for social engagement.
chr_social_associations.statement: In {location}, there were {value} social associations per 10,000 population.

chr.name: County Health Rankings
chr.organization: University of Wisconsin Population Health Institute
chr.description: The County Health Rankings & Roadmaps program is a collaboration between the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation and the University of Wisconsin Population Health Institute. The annual County Health Rankings measure vital health factors, including health behaviors, clinical care, social and economic factors, and the physical environment, for nearly every county in the United States. Data are compiled from dozens of national sources and standardized to enable county-level comparisons within and across states. The 2025 data release covers all 50 states and the District of Columbia at the county, state, and national level. Most measures reflect multi-year averages to improve statistical reliability for smaller counties.
chr.restrictions: Public domain. County Health Rankings data is produced by the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation and the University of Wisconsin Population Health Institute and is freely available for public use. Attribution is appreciated: 'County Health Rankings & Roadmaps, a Robert Wood Johnson Foundation program, www.countyhealthrankings.org.'
